input.background: Let us return to the birth weight illustrative data concerning 10 SIDS cases. We have established that \bar{x}=2890.5 grams and s=720.0.
input.question: Confidence intervals for \mu at 90 \% level of confidence will be calculated.

output.answer: The standard error of the mean SE_{\bar{x}}=\frac{720}{\sqrt{10}}=227.684. df=10-1=9. For 90 \% confidence, use t_{9,95}=1.833 [Table C], so the 90 \% confidence interval for \mu is \bar{x} \pm\left(t_{n-1,1-\xi}\right)\left(SE_{\bar{x}}\right)=2890.5 \pm (1.833)(227.684)=2890.5 \pm 417.3=(2473.2 to 3307.8) grams.

x_bar.description: Sample mean of birth weight in grams.

s.description: Sample standard deviation of birth weight in grams.

n.description: Sample size, representing the number of SIDS cases.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level for the interval estimation.
